title: CC narrative review S05 panel a1

payload.notes: B04 fully delivered: novelty gate as literal gate-hall, 14/20 scoreboard + SURVIVES verdict + DDL T-18:55 all baked and correct (expected_literals 14/20/SURVIVES/DUP all present); the WHY of the burn is self-evident via the large foreground DUP card with its small 'arXiv 2024' note — someone got there first, and the panel says so without leaning on the caption ('6 个想法当场烧掉——早有人做过了。'). Both intended bubbles match the IR text exactly (reviewer terse '够新，留下。' / executor say '我那六张牌啊……'), and the role split carries the beat in one read: the reviewer stamps SURVIVES at the console with calm authority while the executor watches his six cards burn — shoulders shrink in a small deflate, then steady into a tempered half-bounce as the fourteen green survivors line up, landing the 'first time reality trims him' step of the energy ladder (clearly below his S02 hand-off MAX and S04 card-fan jumps). The literature-brick gate wall with only 'PRIOR ART' and 'STRUCTURED OUTPUTS' readable keeps the dark-cyber world contract alive, and the '</>' mug stays a quiet environment prop rather than stealing motif weight. COMPOSITION (STORY) 4/5: the gate-hall packs many stations (queue → gate → burned DUP pile → 14 survivors → scoreboard + stamp + DDL chip), and on first read the scoreboard cluster competes briefly with the large foreground DUP card for the eye before the red-ash-vs-green-glow contrast sorts the path; the core beat stays legible, so the deduction is half-structural, not beat-level. NARRATIVE VERDICT: PASS on story (no beat-level rejection). Visual identity-lock / artifact severity / safezone / baked-text quality are NOT my call — left to the visual reviewers (Gemini + Codex).
